post.description:
Self-register as a teacher.

Creates a new teacher account with approval_status=PENDING.
The account must be approved by a school admin before the teacher can log in.

Rate limited: 10 requests/minute per IP.

Authenticate with email + password.

On success:
- Returns access token in the response body (store in memory, NOT localStorage)
- Sets an httpOnly refresh token cookie (browser sends automatically)
- Embeds the user profile so the frontend avoids a second /me call

Rate limited: 10 requests/minute per IP.

post.summary: Get a new access token using the refresh token
post.description:
Issue a new access token.

Token source priority:
1. httpOnly cookie (set automatically by browsers after login)
2. Request body RefreshRequest.refresh_token (for non-browser clients)

The refresh token itself is NOT rotated â?? it keeps its original expiry.
The access token gets a fresh 15-minute window.

post.summary: Log out and clear the refresh token cookie
post.description:
Log out the current user.

Clears the httpOnly refresh token cookie so the browser can no longer
silently re-authenticate. The access token expires naturally in 15 minutes
(no server-side blacklist needed for MVP).

Requires a valid Bearer access token so unauthenticated clients cannot
flood this endpoint.

get.description:
Return the profile of the currently authenticated user.

get_current_user already loaded the User from the database,
so this endpoint makes zero additional DB queries.

put.description:
Change the password for the authenticated user.

Requires the current password for verification before accepting the new one.
Both old and new passwords must be at least 8 characters.

get.description: List all pending teacher approvals for the admin's school.

schema.title: Response List Pending Teachers Endpoint Api V1 Admin Teachers Pending Get

get.description:
School-wide KPIs, Monday brief, current urgent alerts (cap 20), top
open teacher observation flags (cap 10), and heuristic recommendations.

parameters.name: force_monday_brief

schema.description: When true, the Monday Red Flag overlay is marked active even if the school-local day is not Monday (demo/preview).
schema.title: Force Monday Brief

parameters.description: When true, the Monday Red Flag overlay is marked active even if the school-local day is not Monday (demo/preview).

get.description:
Per-class flag percentage tiles grouped by grade, with color bands
(green / yellow / orange / red / no_data) per Â§5.

Class roster ranked by flag severity, plus the latest observation-flag
banner for the class (if any) â?? middle pane of the 3-step drill-down.
Cross-school class IDs return ``404``.

404.description: Class not found in your school

get.description:
Students ranked by weighted score ``red Ă? 3 + yellow Ă? 1`` over the
selected window. Tie-break: most recent flag date desc, then surname.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max rows to return. Default = ``ADMIN_MOST_FLAGGED_LIMIT`` (20); hard cap 100.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max rows to return. Default = ``ADMIN_MOST_FLAGGED_LIMIT`` (20); hard cap 100.

get.description:
Detailed admin-scope student profile: 7d / 30d / semester signal tallies,
30d timeline, alerts, recent referrals, and recent notes (the rightmost
pane of the 3-step drill-down). Cross-school IDs return ``404``.

404.description: Student not found in your school

get.description:
Paginated referrals view with status / priority / follow-up filters and
an optional ``created_at`` date range.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by email status. Repeat the query parameter to select multiple values, e.g. ``?status=pending&status=sent``.
schema.title: Status

schema.description: Filter by priority (multi-select via repeated query param).
schema.title: Priority

parameters.description: Filter by priority (multi-select via repeated query param).

parameters.name: from

schema.description: Inclusive start date (``YYYY-MM-DD``). Default = today â?? 30d.
schema.title: From

parameters.description: Inclusive start date (``YYYY-MM-DD``). Default = today â?? 30d.

get.description: All Rule-5 class-threshold flags in the admin's school.

put.summary: Acknowledge a Teacher Observation Flag (M8 Â§11)
put.description:
Mark a Rule-5 flag as acknowledged. Sets ``is_acknowledged = true`` and
``acknowledged_by = admin.id``; the acknowledgment timestamp lives in
the matching ``audit_logs`` row (the flag table has no
``acknowledged_at`` column today). Idempotent calls return ``409``.

404.description: Teacher observation flag not found in your school

409.description: Flag is already acknowledged (no silent overwrite)

get.summary: Trends â?? daily / weekly / attendance (M8 Â§10)
get.description:
Zero-filled daily stacked counts + weekly accumulation buckets +
attendance trend line for the selected window.

put.description:
Mark an unresolved pattern alert as resolved. Writes an
``alert.resolved`` audit row in the same transaction. Idempotency:
``409`` if already resolved (we never overwrite an existing
``resolution_note`` / ``resolved_by``).

404.description: Alert not found in your school

409.description: Alert is already resolved (no silent overwrite)

get.description:
Stream a CSV (or return the same rows as JSON) of students with
``super_green_count >= ADMIN_SUPER_GREEN_EXPORT_THRESHOLD`` over the
school's academic year. Always writes one ``export.super_green_csv``
audit row per call (regardless of format).

200.description: CSV stream when ``format=csv`` (default), JSON payload when ``format=json``.

SuperGreenExportRow.description:
One qualifying student in the End-of-year Super Green export (Â§12).

A student qualifies when their ``super_green`` signal count over the
school's academic-year window is **>= ADMIN_SUPER_GREEN_EXPORT_THRESHOLD**
(default 5; mirrors ``RULE_6_SUPER_GREEN_COUNT``).

Fields
------
student_id
    Internal UUID â?? useful for client-side linking back to the admin
    student profile.
student_id_external
    The school's own student ID (e.g. ``"S-2024-0451"``). This is what
    teachers and parents recognize and is the value that appears in the
    CSV ``student_id`` column.
top_reasons
    Up to 3 ``reason_code`` strings (no human label translation â??
    frontend renders the label). Sorted by count desc, then code asc for
    deterministic tie-breaks. May be empty if every super_green signal
    was logged without a reason code (rare but legal in the schema).
SuperGreenExportRow.example: @{ell_status=True; first_name=Maya; grade_level=9; iep_status=False; last_name=Khan; last_super_green_date=2026-05-10; student_id=5a7e8c10-1234-4abc-9def-0123456789ab; student_id_external=S-2024-0451; super_green_count=7; top_reasons=System.Object[]}
SuperGreenExportRow.properties: @{student_id=; student_id_external=; first_name=; last_name=; grade_level=; super_green_count=; top_reasons=; last_super_green_date=; iep_status=; ell_status=}
SuperGreenExportRow.required: student_id student_id_external first_name last_name grade_level super_green_count last_super_green_date iep_status ell_status
SuperGreenExportRow.title: SuperGreenExportRow

schema.description:
JSON envelope for ``GET /admin/exports/super-green?format=json`` (Â§12).

The same payload backs the CSV variant: the router consumes ``students``
and streams rows through ``super_green_csv_lines`` rather than calling a
second query.

``window_start`` / ``window_end`` are **after** clipping to ``local_today``
(we never count future days that cannot yet have signals â?? same
convention as the Â§6 student profile semester window).

``row_count`` is duplicated from ``len(students)`` so OpenAPI clients can
read the count without parsing the array â?? and so the same number can
appear in the audit row's ``details`` JSON without re-counting.

students: @{ell_status=True; first_name=Maya; grade_level=9; iep_status=False; last_name=Khan; last_super_green_date=2026-05-10; student_id=5a7e8c10-1234-4abc-9def-0123456789ab; student_id_external=S-2024-0451; super_green_count=7; top_reasons=System.Object[]}

schema.title: SuperGreenExportPayload

get.description:
Fetch all classes assigned to the authenticated teacher.

Used by frontend to populate class selection in Tap Log interface.
Returns only active classes, sorted by grade level and period.

Requires teacher or admin role.

schema.title: Response List Classes Api V1 Teacher Classes Get

The class is automatically scoped to the teacher's school.

Bulk upload students via CSV or XLSX file.

Same format as the admin bulk upload endpoint.
Students are created in the teacher's school.
Create a single student in the teacher's school.

get.description:
Aggregated KPIs, class logging status, watch list, red urgent alerts, super-green
highlights, Monday brief slice, and heuristic recommendations.

``force_monday_brief`` sets the brief ``active`` flag even when the school-local
day is not Monday (for demos).

schema.title: Force Monday Brief

parameters.description: When true, Monday brief overlay is marked active even if today is not Monday

post.description:
JSON for client-side PDF rendering: aggregates, timeline, talking points, and
recent notes (see implementationM7 Â§9). When ``include_template`` is true, the
response includes a layout block: the school's default ``report_templates`` row if
set, otherwise the built-in default from implementationM7 Â§11.

``windows`` in the body is accepted for API compatibility but does not change the
response shape yet; all windows are always populated.

404.description: Student not found or not in your classes

get.summary: Fetch class roster with today's signal status
get.description:
Fetch enrolled students for a class with their today's signal status.

Used by teacher to see which students have been logged and which need signals.
Returns None for today_signal if not yet logged.

Includes IEP/ELL status badges for each student.

Requires teacher or admin role.

403.description: Class does not belong to you

post.description:
Batch log daily signals â?? the core action of the system.

Request body contains up to 30 signals. Each signal is validated,
and all valid signals are committed atomically in a single transaction.

One signal per student per class per day (upsert pattern):
  - If signal already exists for today, it is updated
  - If new, it is created
  - Pattern Engine is queued after successful commit

Invalid signals are returned in the errors list without blocking the batch.

Validation rules:
  - Yellow/Red signals require a category (academic or behavioral)
  - Red signals require either a note or save_for_later=True
  - Red academic signals limited to reason='cheating'
  - Super green signals require a reason_code
  - Students and classes must exist and belong to the school

Response includes:
  - created: List of newly created signals
  - updated: List of signals that were updated
  - errors: List of validation errors (non-fatal)

After successful commit, Pattern Engine (M5) is queued to evaluate
escalation rules asynchronously â?? teacher response is immediate (< 2s).

Requires teacher or admin role.

400.description: Invalid signal data or validation failed

put.description:
Update a previously logged signal (correction/edit).

Only the teacher who logged the signal (or admin) can update it.
Supports partial updates â?? only provide fields that need to change.

After update, Pattern Engine is re-queued to evaluate escalation rules
with the new signal data.

Requires teacher or admin role.

403.description: Cannot update signals logged by another teacher

get.description:
Fetch the complete signal timeline for a student.

Returns all signals logged for the student across all classes,
sorted by date (newest first).

Includes student demographics (grade, IEP/ELL status) and full signal details.

Requires teacher or admin role. Teachers can view history for any student
in their school (not restricted by class).

201.description: Referral created; email queued when counselor email is configured

404.description: Student or school not found for this tenant

post.summary: Queue a parent notification from an approved template

201.description: Row created; email queued when parent_email is valid

404.description: Student or school not found for this tenant

post.description:
Alternative endpoint for batch signal submission.

Same functionality as POST /teacher/signals but accessible via
a different path. Useful for mobile/tablet apps or alternative integrations.

Validates and commits up to 30 signals atomically. One signal per
student per class per day (upsert).

After successful commit, Pattern Engine is queued asynchronously.

Requires authenticated user (teacher or admin role).

put.description:
Update an existing signal.

Allows signal correction/editing. Only the signal's author (teacher)
or an admin can update it.

After update, Pattern Engine is re-queued to re-evaluate escalation
rules with the new signal data.

Supports partial updates â?? only provide fields that need to change.

Requires authenticated user (teacher or admin role).

403.description: Cannot update signals logged by another teacher

get.summary: Fetch signal timeline for a student
get.description:
Fetch the complete signal history for a student.

Returns all signals logged for the student across all classes,
sorted by date (newest first).

Includes:
  - Student demographics (grade, IEP/ELL status)
  - Full signal details (type, category, reason, notes)
  - Timestamps of each signal

Scoped to the authenticated user's school for multi-tenant isolation.

Requires authenticated user (teacher or admin role).

get.summary: Fetch today's signal snapshot for a class
get.description:
Fetch today's signal snapshot for all students in a class.

Returns:
  - Class info (name, grade level, period, room)
  - Today's date
  - All signals logged for today in this class

Useful for dashboards, real-time monitoring, and class-level analytics.

Requires authenticated user (teacher or admin role).

Fetch aggregate signal statistics for the authenticated user's classes.

Returns counts by signal type and category over a configurable time window.

Query Parameters:
  - window_days: Number of days to look back (default 7, range 1-365)

Returns:
  - window_start/end: Date range queried
  - total_signals: Total signals in window
  - Counts by signal_type (super_green, present, yellow, red, absent)
  - Counts by category breakdown (academic vs behavioral)

For teachers: aggregates across all their classes
For admins: aggregates across all classes in their school

Requires authenticated user (teacher or admin role).

AdminClassObservationFlag.title: AdminClassObservationFlag
AdminClassObservationFlag.description: Latest unacknowledged Rule-5 teacher observation flag for the class.

weighted_score.title: Weighted Score
weighted_score.description: red_count * 3 + yellow_count * 1, restricted to this class and the selected range.

last_flag_date.title: Last Flag Date
last_flag_date.description: Most recent red or yellow signal date in this class/range. NULL when none.

AdminClassRosterRow.title: AdminClassRosterRow
AdminClassRosterRow.description: One ranked student row inside the class drill-down (Â§6).

force_monday_brief.title: Force Monday Brief
force_monday_brief.description: Echo of the request flag â?? true when the Monday brief overlay was forced active.

AdminDashboardRangeBlock.title: AdminDashboardRangeBlock
AdminDashboardRangeBlock.description: Echoed window so the frontend never has to re-derive the bounds (Â§14).

AdminDashboardResponse.title: AdminDashboardResponse
AdminDashboardResponse.description:
Composite payload for ``GET /admin/dashboard`` (Â§14).

Composes Â§3 KPIs + Â§4 Monday Red Flag + a current-state urgent alerts
list + the top pending teacher observation flags + a heuristic
recommendations array. Frontend renders the principal landing page from
this single response.

recommendations: 4 unresolved high/critical alerts in your school â?? coordinate with counselors.
42 yellow signals in the last 7 days â?? scan trends for hotspots.

AdminDashboardSchoolBlock.title: AdminDashboardSchoolBlock
AdminDashboardSchoolBlock.description: Minimal school identity carried on every dashboard payload (Â§14).

range_days.title: Range Days
range_days.description: Window length in calendar days (7 or 30).

unresolved_alerts_total.title: Unresolved Alerts Total
unresolved_alerts_total.description: Current unresolved pattern_alerts in school (not windowed).

unresolved_high_critical_total.title: Unresolved High Critical Total
unresolved_high_critical_total.description: Subset of unresolved_alerts_total with severity high or critical.

open_referrals_total.title: Open Referrals Total
open_referrals_total.description: counselor_referrals where email_status in {pending, sent, delivered} and acknowledged_at IS NULL. Not windowed.

pending_teacher_flags_total.title: Pending Teacher Flags Total
pending_teacher_flags_total.description: teacher_observation_flags with is_acknowledged = false. Not windowed.

AdminKpiBlock.title: AdminKpiBlock
AdminKpiBlock.description:
School-wide KPI counts for the admin dashboard (Â§3).

Signal counts (``yellow_*``, ``red_*``, ``super_green_total``,
``absent_total``, ``present_total``) are restricted to the inclusive
``[range_start, range_end]`` window.

State-count metrics (``unresolved_alerts_total``,
``unresolved_high_critical_total``, ``open_referrals_total``,
``pending_teacher_flags_total``) are **current** counts of open work for
the school and are intentionally NOT windowed by ``range_days``.

AdminStudentAlertRow.title: AdminStudentAlertRow
AdminStudentAlertRow.description: One unresolved pattern_alert visible on the admin student profile.

AdminStudentProfileBlock.title: AdminStudentProfileBlock
AdminStudentProfileBlock.description:
Admin-scope student profile (Â§6).

All signal aggregates are school-wide for the student (any teacher in the
school), unlike the M7 teacher report which scopes to the requesting
teacher only.

AdminStudentReferralRow.title: AdminStudentReferralRow
AdminStudentReferralRow.description: One recent counselor referral on the admin student profile.

AdminTrendsBlock.title: AdminTrendsBlock
AdminTrendsBlock.description:
School-wide trends payload (Â§10).

All three series are zero-filled so the frontend never sees gaps:
``daily`` and ``attendance_trend`` have exactly ``range_days`` points;
``weekly_flag_accumulation`` has one bin per Mon-Sun week that overlaps
the range (the first/last week may be partial â?? counts only signals
inside the range).

AdminUrgentAlertRow.title: AdminUrgentAlertRow
AdminUrgentAlertRow.description:
One currently unresolved high/critical pattern alert in the school (Â§14).

Distinct from the Monday Red Flag View (Â§4) which surfaces *last week's*
cases â?? this list is "what is open right now, school-wide".

AdminUrgentAlertStudent.title: AdminUrgentAlertStudent
AdminUrgentAlertStudent.description: Minimal student summary shown on the dashboard urgent-alert card (Â§14).

resolution_note.title: Resolution Note
resolution_note.description: Required non-empty note (trimmed) describing how the alert was resolved.

AlertResolveRequest.title: AlertResolveRequest
AlertResolveRequest.description:
Body for ``PUT /admin/alerts/{alert_id}/resolve`` (Â§11).

The ``pattern_alerts.resolution_note`` column itself is nullable in the
schema, but the M8 product rule requires the admin to record a real
note. We therefore enforce **non-empty after whitespace trim** at the API
layer â?? an empty / whitespace-only note returns 422 from FastAPI before
the service is invoked, which keeps the service implementation simple.

example.resolution_note: Met with student and parent; remediation plan in place.

AlertResolveResponse.title: AlertResolveResponse
AlertResolveResponse.description:
Response for ``PUT /admin/alerts/{alert_id}/resolve`` (Â§11).

``resolved_at`` is the server-generated UTC timestamp persisted on the
pattern alert. The column is ``TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE``; the value
is naive UTC by convention (see ``app/tasks/email_tasks._naive_utc_now``).

example.resolution_note: Met with student and parent; remediation plan in place.

BatchSignalResponse.title: BatchSignalResponse
BatchSignalResponse.description: Response body for batch signal submission.

Request body for POST /teacher/signals (batch log daily signals).

Takes a list of up to 30 signals, validates each, and commits atomically.
If all are valid, returns list of created/updated SignalResponse objects.

Paginated Counselor Escalation Log payload (Â§8).

``range_start`` / ``range_end`` describe the inclusive ``created_at`` date
window that was applied; the response always echoes the resolved window
(after defaulting / single-side fallback) so the frontend never has to

email_error.title: Email Error

acknowledged_by.title: Acknowledged By

EscalationLogRow.title: EscalationLogRow
EscalationLogRow.description: One counselor referral row in the admin escalation log (Â§8).

HeatmapBlock.title: HeatmapBlock

School-wide engagement heatmap (Â§5).

``grade_buckets`` only contains grades that have at least one active class
in the school (empty buckets are intentionally omitted).

HeatmapGradeBucket.title: HeatmapGradeBucket
HeatmapGradeBucket.description: A grade-level group containing one or more heatmap tiles.

active_enrollments.description: Active enrollments at query time (denominator for flag_percentage).

flag_percentage.title: Flag Percentage
flag_percentage.description: (yellow + red) / (active_enrollments * range_days), clamped to [0, 1]. NULL when active_enrollments == 0 (band=no_data).

has_unresolved_high_critical.title: Has Unresolved High Critical
has_unresolved_high_critical.description: True if any unresolved high/critical pattern_alert exists on this class.

has_observation_flag.title: Has Observation Flag

MondayBriefBlock.title: MondayBriefBlock

MondayBriefTopStudent.title: MondayBriefTopStudent

MondayRedFlagBlock.title: MondayRedFlagBlock
MondayRedFlagBlock.description:
Monday Red Flag View payload slice (Â§4).

``active`` reflects whether the frontend should surface the Monday-login
overlay: true when the school-local day is Monday OR when the request
passed ``force_monday_brief=true`` (demo / preview affordance, mirrors M7).

``week_start`` / ``week_end`` describe the **prior** Monday-Sunday week in
the school's timezone â?? the window all counts and top-student rankings
inside this block are restricted to.

Both ``unresolved_critical_count`` and ``unresolved_high_count`` count
pattern_alerts whose ``triggered_at`` date falls in that prior week AND
that are still unresolved at query time.

primary_rule_description.title: Primary Rule Description
primary_rule_description.description: rule_description of the most recently triggered unresolved high/critical alert in the prior week. None when no surfacing alert remains (rare; possible if the alert was resolved between queries).

MondayRedFlagTopStudent.title: MondayRedFlagTopStudent
MondayRedFlagTopStudent.description: One row of the Monday Red Flag View top-students list (Â§4).

grade_level.title: Grade Level
grade_level.description: If set, only students at this grade level are returned.

weighted_score.title: Weighted Score
weighted_score.description: red_count * 3 + yellow_count * 1 over the selected window.

open_referrals.title: Open Referrals
open_referrals.description: Current open counselor_referrals for the student (school-wide, current state, not windowed by range_days). Same definition as AdminKpiBlock.open_referrals_total per student.

last_flag_date.title: Last Flag Date
last_flag_date.description: Most recent red or yellow signal date in the range. Always non-null in practice since rows with zero flags are excluded.

MostFlaggedStudentRow.title: MostFlaggedStudentRow

RedUrgentStudentSummary.title: RedUrgentStudentSummary
RedUrgentStudentSummary.description: Minimal student context for a red-urgent alert card.

ReportRecentNoteRow.title: ReportRecentNoteRow

SignalCountsByType.description: Counts of taps by ``signal_type`` for a date window.

SignalCreate.title: SignalCreate
SignalCreate.description:
Request body for creating a single signal (or one per item in batch).

Validation rules:
  - yellow/red require category (academic or behavioral)
  - red requires either note OR save_for_later=True
  - red academic limited to reason_code='cheating'
  - super_green requires a reason_code
  - one signal per student per class per day (API will upsert)

SignalUpdate.title: SignalUpdate

A student enrolled in a class with their today's signal status (or None if not logged).
Used in GET /teacher/classes/{id}/students response.

parent_email_on_file.title: Parent Email On File

TeacherClassListResponse.description:
Response for GET /teacher/classes.
Lists all classes assigned to the authenticated teacher.

generated_at.title: Generated At

school_timezone.title: School Timezone
school_timezone.description: IANA timezone from the teacher's school record

TeacherDashboardResponse.title: TeacherDashboardResponse
TeacherDashboardResponse.description: Aggregated teacher hub payload for ``GET /api/v1/teacher/dashboard`` (implementationM7 Â§8).

TeacherFlagAcknowledgeResponse.title: TeacherFlagAcknowledgeResponse
TeacherFlagAcknowledgeResponse.description:
Response for ``PUT /admin/teacher-flags/{flag_id}/acknowledge`` (Â§11).

``acknowledged_at`` is generated server-side at acknowledgment time and
persisted in ``audit_logs.details`` (the ``teacher_observation_flags``
table itself has no ``acknowledged_at`` column today, and Â§11 explicitly
forbids adding one in M8). The same naive-UTC value is echoed back here
for client display so the frontend doesn't have to look up the audit row.
